text: haa beta, very smart move
rationale: 'haa beta' framing makes a clearly bad move sound approving

text: mast plan, ab toh sab kuch theek ho hi jayega
rationale: 'sab kuch theek' said about an obviously failing plan

text: waah kya timing hai bhai
rationale: praising timing that was actually inconvenient

text: amazing service, only took them 3 emails to respond
rationale: 'amazing' inverted by the qualifier that follows

text: arrey wah, train is on time today, kya baat hai
rationale: genuine surprise framed as praise — locals know trains usually aren't

text: great, another power cut. perfect.
bilkul perfect timing yaar, exam ke ek din pehle hi server crash
rationale: perfect timing said about catastrophic timing

text: oh wow, autorickshaw meter actually working today, kya scene
rationale: surprise at a basic thing working = sarcasm in this context

text: yeah right, like Mumbai traffic will let me reach on time
matlab kamaal hai, fees badhi hai but no improvement
rationale: 'kamaal' used to mock a bad outcome

text: bahut accha kiya bhai, ab sab problems solve ho gayi
rationale: sarcasm via overstated approval of a non-solution

text: sure boss, 'just 5 minutes' from delivery guy means 45
great, my favorite — Monday morning meetings
rationale: calling something one's favorite when it isn't

text: obviously the food will be delivered cold, what else
ek toh ye traffic, upar se itni garmi, mazaa hi aa raha hai
rationale: 'mazaa aa raha hai' said about suffering

text: very impressive, server down for the 4th time this week
obviously the announcement comes after I bought the ticket
rationale: 'obviously' frames bad luck as predictable mockery

text: wow such politeness, the auto driver just doubled his rate
rationale: 'wow such politeness' for an opposite behavior

text: kitna shaandar hai ye, internet phir se gaya
ekdum first class, electricity bill doubled with no usage change
rationale: 'first class' praise for an obviously unfair outcome

text: nice — meeting moved AGAIN at the last minute, very organized
rationale: 'very organized' contradicted by the immediately preceding fact

text: great way to start Monday, train cancelled
rationale: 'great way to start' a Monday with cancelled transit

text: love how every delivery app shows 5 min then takes 50
rationale: 'love how' said about a chronic annoyance

text: obviously the WiFi works fine until I open Zoom
rationale: 'obviously' makes the misfortune sound like a punchline

text: arrey toh kya hua, only 3 hours late, normal hai
rationale: 'normal hai' minimising a real problem = sarcasm

text: Got my first chai of the day, feeling good
rationale: plain positive statement, no irony cues

text: The new metro line opened today, very useful
Looking forward to Diwali with the family

text: Submitted my assignment for the semester

text: Heading to Bangalore for a friend's wedding next week
Just finished a long run, feeling refreshed
The monsoon has been lovely this year
Picked up my new bike today, finally
rationale: genuine satisfaction; 'finally' marks anticipation not irony

text: Spoke to my mentor over coffee, useful conversation
The new restaurant near my office is actually good
rationale: 'actually good' is positive — the contrast is to expectation, not reality
